stem: A pump fills a swimming pool at a rate of 150 gallons per minute. How many gallons of water does the pump deliver in 2 hours and 30 minutes?
explanation:
The pump runs for 2 hours and 30 minutes. First, convert the total time to minutes, then multiply by the pumping rate.

Step 1: Convert time to minutes.
$$2 \text{ hr} \times \frac{60 \text{ min}}{1 \text{ hr}} + 30 \text{ min} = 120 \text{ min} + 30 \text{ min} = 150 \text{ min}$$

Step 2: Multiply by the rate.
$$150 \frac{\text{gal}}{\text{min}} \times 150 \text{ min} = 22{,}500 \text{ gal}$$

The pump delivers **22,500 gallons**.

distractorRationale.A: Choice A (375) results from failing to convert hours to minutes — the student uses 2.5 hours directly as if the units were already compatible with a per-minute rate: $150 \times 2.5 = 375$. The student multiplies the rate (gallons per minute) by a time value still expressed in hours.
distractorRationale.B: Choice B (18,000) results from converting only the 2 hours to minutes (getting 120 min) and ignoring the additional 30 minutes: $150 \times 120 = 18{,}000$. This is an early-stop error where the student omits part of the total time.
distractorRationale.C: Choice C (22,500) is the correct answer.
distractorRationale.D: Choice D (27,000) results from treating the 30 minutes as a full hour, converting 2 hours 30 minutes to 180 minutes instead of 150 minutes: $150 \times 180 = 27{,}000$. The student rounds 30 minutes up to 60 minutes, adding an extra half-hour of pumping time.
